task.title: Register in-memory tool adapters
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/551 by adding a process-local registry that owns adapters, discovers permission metadata deterministically, and dispatches by stable tool ID.
acceptance_criteria: Registration owns adapters and rejects duplicate IDs without replacing either adapter.
Metadata enumeration is deterministic by stable ID and produces no invocation effects.
Known IDs delegate through the existing permission protocol and unknown IDs fail before authorization.
No reload, removal, replacement, persistence, concrete adapter, or second authorization path is introduced.

attempts.summary: Scoped the smallest registry ownership boundary.
attempts.diagnostic: Runtime tool orchestration was premature while extension-provided adapters had no kernel-owned directory. Reload and persistent lifecycle policy were also unnecessary for initial process-local dispatch.
attempts.patch: Execution issue 551 fixed stable-ID registration, deterministic metadata, and permission-checked lookup while deferring runtime parsing and extension lifecycle policy.

attempts.summary: Established RED with registry integration tests.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused target failed with unresolved imports because ToolRegistry, ToolMetadata, and typed registry errors did not exist.
attempts.patch: Kept tests for ID ordering, all effect classes, duplicate rejection, original-adapter preservation, allow, deny, sourced failure, and unknown-ID short circuiting.

attempts.diagnostic: A BTreeMap owns boxed adapters by ToolId. Registration rejects duplicates, metadata clones only stable permission fields in sorted order, and invocation resolves before delegating to invoke_tool.
attempts.patch: Added the minimal registry, metadata, and error APIs in tool.rs; added focused integration coverage and documented explicit lifecycle non-goals.

attempts.summary: Verified the slice and merged despite unavailable automated review capacity.
attempts.diagnostic: Pinned just verify, registry tests, and rustdoc with warnings denied passed, and independent pre-commit review found no actionable issue. Rust quality gate run 30055540137 succeeded. CodeRabbit reported an adaptive review limit rather than line review.
attempts.patch: Pull request 552 squash-merged as 6ae809d7803e660b4ed272d352d18e9147fa962b and closed issue 551.

outcome.summary: Vela can now own, enumerate, and permission-dispatch extension-provided in-memory tool adapters by stable ID without introducing lifecycle or persistence policy.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test tool_registry
Independent pre-commit review # no actionable findings

lessons: A deterministic in-memory registry is enough to establish adapter ownership before extension reload or persistence policy exists.
Duplicate registration should fail without replacement so identity ownership cannot change silently.
Metadata discovery must not invoke adapters or bypass the per-invocation permission path.
Using one existing invocation function avoids creating a registry-specific authorization protocol.
